function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?

dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 5:  <statement> for (size_t k = 0; k < i; k++) { </statement> is reached, the statement line 9:  <statement> printf("*"); </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 4:  <statement> for ( i = 0; i < 5; i++) { </statement> dominate line 13:  <statement> return 0; </statement>?
data_dependency: Does variable i at line 4: <statement>   for ( i = 0; i < 5; i++) { </statement> determine the value stored in variable j at line 8: <statement>     for ( j = i; j < 5; j++) { </statement>?

Does the value of variable j at line 8: <statement>     for ( j = i; j < 5; j++) { </statement> depend on the value of variable k at line 5: <statement>     for (size_t k = 0; k < i; k++) { </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'j' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'printf("*");' be considered dead code?
